steps.title: Identify the linear scale factor
steps.content: Each dimension increases by 40%, so it's multiplied by 1 + 0.40 = 1.4.

steps.title: Apply area scaling and compute the new area
steps.content:
When all linear dimensions scale by factor k, area scales by k². With k = 1.4:
$$\text{Area scale factor} = (1.4)^2 = 1.96$$
$$\text{New area} = 3,500 \times 1.96 = 6,860 \text{ square inches}$$
That confirms A is correct.
